creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
Greetings,

I am sorry to hear of the troubles, but I cannot reproduce the problem
here (9.1-RELEASE amd64).

If I try e2fsck'ing an UFS2 SU+J file system, I get:

$ sudo env LC_ALL=C e2fsck -n /dev/label/usr
e2fsck 1.42.7 (21-Jan-2013)
Warning!  /dev/label/usr is mounted.
ext2fs_open2: Bad magic number in super-block
e2fsck: Superblock invalid, trying backup blocks...
e2fsck: Bad magic number in super-block while trying to open /dev/label/usr

The superblock could not be read or does not describe a correct ext2
filesystem.  If the device is valid and it really contains an ext2
filesystem (and not swap or ufs or something else), then the superblock
is corrupt, and you might try running e2fsck with an alternate superblock:
    e2fsck -b 8193 <device>


And an exit status of 8.


And just in case you're wondering:

$ sudo env LC_ALL=C tunefs -p /dev/label/usr
tunefs: POSIX.1e ACLs: (-a)                                disabled
tunefs: NFSv4 ACLs: (-N)                                   enabled
tunefs: MAC multilabel: (-l)                               disabled
tunefs: soft updates: (-n)                                 enabled
tunefs: soft update journaling: (-j)                       enabled
tunefs: gjournal: (-J)                                     disabled
tunefs: trim: (-t)                                         disabled
tunefs: maximum blocks per file in a cylinder group: (-e)  2048
tunefs: average file size: (-f)                            16384
tunefs: average number of files in a directory: (-s)       64
tunefs: minimum percentage of free space: (-m)             8%
tunefs: optimization preference: (-o)                      time
tunefs: volume label: (-L)                                 usr


Please follow up with a way to reproduce the problem, else we must close
this report.

Thank you.
